cpj45.desc: High strength cement for reinforced concrete, floor slabs and civil engineering works. The benchmark choice for demanding projects in Morocco.

cpj55.desc: Ultra high strength cement for major civil engineering works and large infrastructure projects. Exceptional performance for the most demanding construction sites.

clinker.title: Clinker — Our Raw Material
clinker.desc: Clinker is the main component of our cement. Sourced from the best cement plants, it guarantees the quality and resistance of our finished products. Our grinding center transforms this raw material into superior quality cement.
clinker.feature1Desc: Each clinker delivery is analyzed in the laboratory to guarantee its chemical and physical compliance
clinker.feature2Desc: Our ball mills ensure optimal grinding fineness for superior mechanical performance
clinker.feature3Desc: A permanent stock of clinker allows us to ensure continuous production and short delivery times

wizard.subtitle: Answer 2 questions to find the right cement for your project
wizard.step1Question: What type of project are you building?
wizard.step2Question: What load level will the concrete need to support?
wizard.cpj45Recommend: CPJ 45 is ideal for your project. Its 45 MPa resistance covers most residential and commercial needs while offering excellent value for money.
wizard.cpj55Recommend: CPJ 55 is the recommended choice for your project. Its superior 55 MPa resistance guarantees the durability of structures subjected to heavy loads.

process.title: Our Manufacturing Process
process.subtitle: From raw material to finished product, discover each step of our clinker grinding process

step1.title: Clinker Reception
step1.desc: Clinker is received and stored at our Lassargua facility. We ensure rigorous quality control at every delivery, verifying the chemical composition and physical properties of the raw material.

step2.title: Fine Grinding
step2.desc: Clinker is finely ground in our high-performance ball mills. The grinding process guarantees optimal fineness to ensure the mechanical characteristics of the finished cement.

step3.title: Dosing & Additions
step3.desc: Precise dosing of additives (gypsum, limestone, fly ash) guarantees the specific characteristics of each cement type. Each formulation is rigorously controlled by our laboratory.

step4.title: Packaging & Shipping
step4.desc: Cement is packaged in 50 kg bags, big bags or shipped in bulk. Our optimized logistics ensure fast and reliable delivery across Southern Morocco and beyond.

choisir-ciment-projet: Choosing the right cement is a crucial decision for any construction project. The two most common types of cement in Morocco are CPJ 45 and CPJ 55, each with its own characteristics.

CPJ 45, with its compressive strength of 45 MPa at 28 days, is the ideal choice for demanding works: reinforced concrete, foundations, floor slabs and civil engineering structures. Its high initial strength allows for rapid formwork removal.
CPJ 55, ultra-performing, offers superior strength of 55 MPa, ideal for major civil engineering works and large infrastructure projects.
For coastal environments like Dakhla, it is recommended to prioritize high-strength cement for structures exposed to sea spray. Consult our technical team for personalized recommendations.
capacite-500k-tonnes: Dakhla Aménagement S.A. continues to strengthen its position as a major player in the cement industry in the Southern Provinces. With constantly growing production capacity, our grinding center meets the increasing demand of the market.

Continuous investment in our equipment and processes allows us to maintain a consistent level of quality while increasing our production volumes. Our high-performance ball mills guarantee optimal grinding fineness.
This extended capacity enables us to serve not only the local Dakhla market, but also the entire Southern Moroccan region and neighboring Mauritania, contributing to regional economic development.
normes-ciment-maroc: The Moroccan standard NM 10.1.004 and the European standard EN 197-1 define the specifications that cements marketed in Morocco must comply with. These standards guarantee the quality and safety of structures.
CPJ 45 and CPJ 55 are classified as composite Portland cements (CEM II). They contain Portland clinker and secondary constituents in regulated proportions.

At Dakhla Aménagement, each production batch undergoes exhaustive testing: compressive strength, grinding fineness (Blaine surface), setting time, stability and heat of hydration. No product leaves the factory without a certificate of conformity.
construction-durable-ciment: The cement industry faces the major challenge of reducing its carbon footprint. At Dakhla Aménagement, we invest in advanced energy efficiency technologies and optimized grinding processes.
The use of secondary constituents such as fly ash and blast furnace slag reduces the amount of clinker needed, thereby reducing CO₂ emissions per tonne of cement produced.
Our environmental approach also includes process water recycling, emission filtration in accordance with MARHP standards, and responsible management of industrial waste.
projet-infrastructure-dakhla: Dakhla is experiencing unprecedented infrastructure development. New roads, port extensions, public facilities — these major projects require reliable and quality cement supplies.
Dakhla Aménagement is proud to contribute to these construction sites by supplying CPJ 45 cement for bridges and foundations, and CPJ 55 for civil engineering works and special structures.
Our optimized logistics and proximity to construction sites enable fast deliveries, even for urgent orders. We accompany our clients from order to delivery.
stockage-ciment-chantier: Proper storage of cement on site is essential to preserve its properties. Cement must be protected from moisture, stored on pallets and ideally in a covered, ventilated area.
The shelf life of cement is limited: approximately 3 months for bagged cement stored in good conditions. Beyond that, strength may decrease significantly.
For large quantities, silo storage is recommended. Dakhla Aménagement offers bulk delivery with direct silo filling, guaranteeing optimal product quality until use.
cpj45-vs-cpj55-guide: Choosing between CPJ 45 and CPJ 55 is a central question for any project owner in Morocco. These two composite Portland cements, produced by Dakhla Aménagement, have distinct mechanical characteristics that suit them for different uses. CPJ 45 offers a compressive strength of 45 MPa at 28 days, while CPJ 55 reaches 55 MPa under the same conditions.

CPJ 45 is the versatile cement par excellence. It is perfectly suited for standard masonry, interior and exterior plastering, floor slabs and shallow foundations. Its grinding fineness of 350 m²/kg or more gives it excellent workability, facilitating implementation on site. For standard residential projects, it is the economical and high-performing choice.

CPJ 55, with its superior strength, is recommended for civil engineering works, deep foundations, heavily loaded structures and aggressive environments. In coastal areas like Dakhla, where sea spray accelerates corrosion, CPJ 55 offers increased durability thanks to its denser and less permeable matrix.

In summary, for residential projects and standard masonry, CPJ 45 is the optimal choice in terms of value for money. For bridges, infrastructure and demanding environments, CPJ 55 guarantees superior performance and increased longevity of structures.
calculer-quantite-ciment: Accurately estimating the quantity of cement needed is a crucial step to optimize costs and avoid waste on site. The calculation depends on the type of structure, the concrete or mortar dosage, and the dimensions of the element to be produced. For standard reinforced concrete, the standard dosage is 350 kg of cement per cubic meter of concrete.

For a reinforced concrete slab 10 cm thick and 50 m² in surface area, the volume of concrete needed is 5 m³. With a dosage of 350 kg/m³, 1,750 kg of cement is required, or about 35 bags of 50 kg. For foundations, the dosage can increase to 400 kg/m³, which proportionally increases the amount of cement required.

For masonry walls, the calculation is based on the number of concrete blocks and the jointing mortar. Count approximately 25 kg of cement per m² of wall in 20 cm blocks. For plastering, plan about 15 kg of cement per m² for a 1.5 cm thickness. These ratios allow for a quick estimate for quantitative quotes.

It is recommended to provide an additional 5 to 10% margin to compensate for losses and dosage inaccuracies. Dakhla Aménagement offers a technical advisory service to help you precisely calculate your cement needs according to your project specifications.
beton-arme-maroc: Reinforced concrete is the most widely used construction material in Morocco, combining the compressive strength of concrete and the tensile strength of steel. In Moroccan climatic conditions, characterized by high temperatures and strong evaporation, the implementation of reinforced concrete requires special precautions to guarantee the quality of the structures.

Reinforcement must be dimensioned according to Moroccan seismic regulations (RPS 2011), particularly in the country's seismic zones. The concrete cover of the reinforcement, that is, the minimum distance between the steel surface and the concrete surface, must be at least 3 cm in standard environments and 5 cm in marine or aggressive environments. This cover protects the steel against corrosion.

Pouring concrete in hot climates requires increased vigilance. The temperature of fresh concrete should not exceed 30°C to avoid too rapid setting. It is recommended to pour early in the morning or late afternoon, to moisten the formwork before pouring, and to protect the fresh concrete from sun and wind. Curing, which consists of keeping the concrete moist for at least 7 days, is essential to achieve the specified strengths.

For projects in Morocco, the choice of cement is crucial. Dakhla Aménagement's CPJ 45 is perfectly suited for standard reinforced concrete structures, while CPJ 55 is recommended for heavily loaded elements and structures in marine environments.
dakhla-pole-developpement: Dakhla is experiencing unprecedented economic transformation, positioned as one of the most dynamic development hubs in Southern Morocco. The city benefits from massive investments in infrastructure: port extension, new express road, special economic zone, and modern public facilities. These structural projects create a growing demand for construction materials.

The Southern Provinces Development Program, launched by His Majesty King Mohammed VI, provides for investments of several billion dirhams. This program includes the construction of roads, hospitals, schools, housing and port facilities. The construction sector is the primary beneficiary of this dynamic, with double-digit annual growth.

Dakhla's geographical position, at the crossroads of North Africa and Sub-Saharan Africa, makes it a strategic logistics hub. The deep-water port and proximity to Mauritania open up export perspectives to countries in the region. Dakhla Aménagement, with its Lassargua grinding center, is at the heart of this dynamic.

For construction companies, Dakhla represents a unique development opportunity. The local availability of quality cement, produced by Dakhla Aménagement, guarantees reliable and competitive supplies, a key success factor for construction projects in the region.
role-gypse-ciment: Gypsum is an essential component of cement manufacturing, although it represents only 3 to 5% of the final composition. Its main role is to regulate the setting time of the cement, without which the concrete would harden almost instantly upon contact with water, making any implementation impossible. Gypsum retards the hydration of tricalcium aluminate (C3A), the clinker compound responsible for rapid setting.

The dosage of gypsum must be precise: insufficient, the cement sets too quickly (flash set); excessive, it can cause abnormal swelling of the concrete and reduce mechanical strengths. The optimal quantity depends on the C3A content of the clinker, the grinding fineness and the ambient temperature. At Dakhla Aménagement, the dosage is rigorously controlled by our laboratory for each production batch.

Gypsum also influences the long-term properties of the cement. An appropriate dosage contributes to the optimal development of mechanical strengths at 7, 28 and 90 days. It also participates in the dimensional stability of the hardened concrete, preventing swelling reactions that could compromise the integrity of the structures.

There are different types of gypsum that can be used: natural gypsum (CaSO4·2H2O), hemihydrate (plaster) and anhydrite. The choice of type and purity of gypsum is an important parameter that the engineers at Dakhla Aménagement master to guarantee the consistency of the quality of our CPJ 45 and CPJ 55 cements.
conditionnement-vrac: Bulk packaging represents the most economical and ecological solution for large-scale construction sites. The cement is delivered by tanker truck and pumped directly into storage silos on site, eliminating the costs and waste associated with packaging. This method represents a significant saving on the unit cost of cement.

In terms of logistics, bulk offers considerable advantages. A tanker truck can transport up to 30 tonnes of cement, compared to 26 tonnes in bags. Unloading time is halved: 30 minutes for a bulk truck compared to over an hour for manual unloading of bags. This efficiency translates into smoother construction sites and less labor devoted to handling.

In terms of quality, bulk better preserves the properties of the cement. Unlike bags that are exposed to moisture and repeated handling, cement in silos remains protected from humidity and contamination. The loss of strength due to aging is also reduced, as cement in silos is generally consumed more quickly than a stock of bags.

Dakhla Aménagement has a fleet of modern tanker trucks equipped with high-pressure pumping systems, capable of supplying silos located several tens of meters in height. Our logistics service ensures bulk delivery throughout Southern Morocco and in Mauritania, with deadlines of 24 to 72 hours depending on the zone.
construction-zone-cotiere: Construction in coastal areas presents specific challenges related to the marine environment. Sea spray, loaded with sodium and magnesium chlorides, penetrates the porosity of the concrete and attacks the metal reinforcement, causing corrosion. This phenomenon, known as accelerated carbonation, is the primary cause of degradation of seaside structures.

In Dakhla, the combination of marine humidity, sandy winds and significant thermal amplitudes creates a particularly aggressive environment for concrete structures. Structures exposed to the northwest, the direction of the prevailing spray-laden winds, are the most vulnerable. It is therefore crucial to choose an appropriate cement and to respect the rules of the art for marine reinforced concrete.

Dakhla Aménagement's CPJ 55 is particularly recommended for structures in coastal areas. Its superior strength produces a denser and less permeable concrete matrix, slowing the penetration of chlorides. Combined with a reinforcement cover of at least 5 cm and a water/cement ratio of less than 0.45, it guarantees optimal durability of maritime structures.

Complementary measures include the use of water-repellent admixtures, the protection of exposed faces with waterproofing coatings, and prolonged curing of fresh concrete. Regular maintenance, including visual inspection of cracks and refurbishment of protective coatings, contributes to significantly extending the lifespan of coastal structures.
10-ans-excellence: In 2025, Dakhla Aménagement S.A. celebrates a decade of existence, a decade marked by major advances for the cement industry of Southern Morocco. Founded in 2015 by visionary Mauritanian investors, the company was born from the conviction that the Dakhla region deserved modern cement infrastructure to support its rapid development.

The early years were devoted to the construction of the Lassargua grinding center, an ambitious project that required an investment of several million dirhams. The choice of the site, close to Dakhla and transport routes, was strategic to ensure rapid delivery to construction sites in the region. The official start of production in 2018 marked the beginning of a new era for the local construction sector.

Over the years, Dakhla Aménagement has constantly invested in improving its equipment and processes. The modernization of mills, the installation of advanced filtration systems, and the creation of a high-performance quality laboratory have made it possible to achieve the most demanding standards. Surpassing the 100,000 tonnes of annual capacity is a testament to this continuous growth.

Today, Dakhla Aménagement is much more than a cement producer. It is an actor committed to the economic and social development of the region, with 35 direct jobs and over 350 indirect jobs. The company looks to the future with ambition, driven by the trust of its customers and the growth dynamic of Southern Morocco.
devenir-revendeur: Dakhla Aménagement's reseller program offers an attractive commercial opportunity for construction material distributors in Southern Morocco. As an approved DAM reseller, you benefit from a 5% discount on public rates, personalized commercial support and the possibility of obtaining a geographical exclusivity zone.

The registration process is simple and quick. Simply contact our sales department by email at contact@ciment-dam.com or by phone at +212 658-265685. Our team will guide you through the registration steps, which include providing administrative documents (trade register, tax identification) and a visit to your storage facilities by our sales manager.

The advantages of the reseller program go beyond the simple tariff discount. You gain access to a recognized brand in the region, marketing materials (brochures, posters, promotional items), and delivery priority during periods of high demand. Our reseller network also benefits from regular technical training on CPJ 45 and CPJ 55 products.

Exclusivity zones are allocated based on the potential of the local market and the reseller's commitment in terms of minimum purchase volume. This exclusivity guarantees the reseller a privileged position in their territory, without direct competition from other DAM distributors. Join our network and become a partner of the reference cement brand in Southern Morocco.
transport-ciment-logistique: Cement transport is a major logistical component that directly influences costs and delivery times. Two packaging modes dominate: bulk, transported by tanker trucks, and bags, transported by flatbed trucks. Each mode has specific advantages and constraints that determine the choice for each type of construction site.

Bulk transport is the most economical for distances under 200 km. Tanker trucks, with a capacity of 20 to 30 tonnes, ensure rapid unloading by pneumatic pumping directly into silos. This mode eliminates the risk of packaging deterioration and reduces product losses. However, it requires a reception silo on the construction site.

Transport in 50 kg bags remains the most common mode for small and medium-sized construction sites. The bags are palletized (40 bags per pallet, or 2 tonnes) and covered for protection against the weather. Unloading can be mechanized (forklift) or manual. Big Bag packaging (1 to 1.5 tonnes) offers an interesting compromise between bulk and individual bags.

Moroccan regulations strictly govern the transport of powdery materials. Trucks must be equipped with anti-dust systems and respect speed limits. Dakhla Aménagement ensures that all its partner carriers comply with these requirements, guaranteeing secure deliveries that comply with current regulations.
rse-communaute-dakhla: Dakhla Aménagement's corporate social responsibility goes well beyond the production of quality cement. Since its creation, the company has been actively committed to the social and economic development of the Dakhla community. This CSR approach is structured around four pillars: local employment, vocational training, social actions and support for community initiatives.

Local employment is the priority of our CSR policy. Of the 35 direct jobs created by Dakhla Aménagement, more than 80% are held by residents of Dakhla and the Southern Provinces. Furthermore, our activity generates more than 350 indirect jobs in transport, distribution and associated services. Each job created in our factory leads to the creation of ten jobs in the local economy.

Vocational training is a pillar of our commitment. We offer practical internships for students from technical schools in Dakhla, industrial safety training for our employees, and awareness workshops on careers in the cement industry. Each year, we welcome about ten interns, several of whom are subsequently hired within our teams.

Our social actions include support for local schools, participation in national solidarity campaigns, and donations to charitable associations in the region. Dakhla Aménagement is also a partner of local authorities for the improvement of community infrastructure. Our conviction is deep: a prosperous company can only exist within a prosperous community.
essais-resistance-ciment: Cement strength tests are fundamental tests that guarantee the quality and conformity of products delivered to the construction site. These tests, standardized by NM 10.1.004 and EN 197-1 standards, measure the mechanical performance of cement at different ages and under different conditions. Compressive strength is the most determining parameter.

The compression test is performed on cylindrical or cubic specimens of standardized mortar, composed of cement, standardized sand and water in strictly defined proportions. The specimens are kept in water at 20°C for 2, 7 and 28 days before being subjected to an increasing load until failure. The compressive strength at 28 days determines the cement class: 45 MPa for CPJ 45 and 55 MPa for CPJ 55.

The flexural tensile test complements the compression measurements. It is performed on 40x40x160 mm mortar prisms, according to EN 196-1. Flexural strength is generally between 6 and 10 MPa at 28 days for composite Portland cements. Although less directly used in structural calculations, this value provides indications on the cohesion of the cementitious matrix.

At Dakhla Aménagement, each production batch undergoes these tests before shipment. Our laboratory equipped with calibrated hydraulic presses and standardized testing machines guarantees the reliability of the results. No product leaves the factory without a certificate of conformity attesting that the measured strengths meet the declared specifications.
big-bag-vs-sacs: The choice of cement packaging is a practical and economic decision that directly impacts the organization of the construction site and construction costs. Dakhla Aménagement offers three formats: 50 kg bags, Big Bags of 1 to 1.5 tonnes, and bulk. Each has advantages and disadvantages that should be carefully evaluated before ordering.

The 50 kg bags are the most common and practical packaging for small and medium-sized construction sites. Easy to handle, they allow precise dosing and can be stored in confined spaces. Their main disadvantage is repetitive manual handling, which requires two workers per bag and generates risks of back injuries. The packaging cost adds about 30 DH per tonne to the price of cement.

Big Bags of 1 to 1.5 tonnes offer an excellent compromise for medium-sized construction sites. They require lifting equipment (crane, forklift) for handling, but considerably reduce the number of unloading operations. A 1-tonne Big Bag replaces 20 bags of 50 kg, dividing handling time by 20. The surcharge compared to bulk is about 80 DH per tonne, but the investment in a silo is not necessary.

In summary, for small construction sites and renovation work, 50 kg bags remain the most flexible solution. For medium-sized sites with lifting equipment, Big Bags optimize logistics. For large construction sites, bulk remains the most economical and efficient solution. Do not hesitate to consult our team to determine the most suitable packaging for your project.
5-erreurs-ciment: The use of cement on site is subject to precise rules, the non-compliance with which can seriously compromise the quality of structures. The first and most common mistake is incorrect dosage. Insufficient dosage reduces mechanical strengths and concrete durability. Excessive dosage is wasteful and can cause shrinkage cracks. Compliance with prescribed dosages (350 kg/m³ for standard reinforced concrete) is imperative.

The second common mistake is excessive water addition. Many workers add water to facilitate implementation, but each liter of excess water reduces concrete strength by about 2 to 3 MPa. The water/cement ratio should never exceed 0.50 for reinforced structures. Prefer the use of a plasticizer to improve workability without compromising strengths.

The third mistake is inadequate cement storage. Cement left on the ground, exposed to moisture or bad weather, degrades quickly. Wet cement sets in lumps and loses a significant portion of its strength. Always store cement on pallets, in a covered and ventilated area, and consume it within 3 months of manufacture.

The fourth and fifth mistakes are insufficient curing and premature formwork removal. Curing, which keeps the concrete moist for at least 7 days, is essential for optimal strength development. Without curing, the 28-day strength can be reduced by 30 to 50%. Premature formwork removal can cause irreversible deformations and cracks. Respect the minimum deadlines: 24 hours for vertical elements, 7 days for floor slabs, and 21 days for spans exceeding 6 meters.

sustainability.title: Sustainability & Environment
sustainability.subtitle: Our commitment to a responsible and sustainable future in the cement industry

engagement.title: Our 2030 Commitments
engagement.desc: Dakhla Aménagement S.A. is committed to reducing its carbon footprint by 30% by 2030. Our environmental roadmap integrates energy efficiency, circular economy and biodiversity preservation at the heart of every stage of our industrial process.

co2.title: CO₂ Reduction
co2.desc: Optimization of our grinding processes and incorporation of secondary constituents to reduce the amount of clinker needed per tonne of cement produced, with a target of -30% emissions by 2030.

energy.title: Energy Efficiency
energy.desc: Installation of solar panels to power our facilities and recovery of waste heat from our mills to optimize our overall energy balance.

circular.title: Circular Economy
circular.desc: Substitution of clinker with mineral additions (fly ash, slag, limestone filler) and recycling of 95% of our industrial waste to reduce the extraction of virgin raw materials.

biodiversity.title: Biodiversity
biodiversity.desc: Continuous environmental monitoring of our Lassargua site and protection of local ecosystems in respect of Saharan flora and fauna.

counters.title: Our Targets in Numbers
counters.subtitle: Measurable ambitions for a concrete impact

actions.title: Our Concrete Actions
actions.subtitle: Tangible measures for a positive environmental impact

solar.title: Solar Panels
solar.desc: Installation of photovoltaic panels on our Lassargua facilities to produce clean energy and reduce our dependence on fossil fuels.

clinker.title: Clinker Substitution
clinker.desc: Incorporation of mineral additions — fly ash, blast furnace slag and limestone filler — to reduce the clinker content and associated CO₂ emissions.

water.title: Water Recycling
water.desc: Closed-loop recycling system for process water enabling 100% of used water to be recycled, reducing our freshwater consumption to zero.

dust.title: Dust Filtration
dust.desc: Installation of advanced filtration systems compliant with MARHP standards, significantly reducing dust and particle emissions into the atmosphere.

sourcing.title: Local Sourcing
sourcing.desc: Priority given to local suppliers to reduce the carbon footprint of transport and support the economic development of the Dakhla region.

training.title: Environmental Training
training.desc: Continuous training program for all our employees on environmental best practices, safety and sustainable development.

compliance.title: Regulatory Compliance
compliance.subtitle: Our commitment to the strictest standards

nm.title: NM 10.1.004
nm.desc: Full compliance with the Moroccan standard governing cement specifications, guaranteeing the quality and safety of our products.

iso.title: ISO 14001
iso.desc: Implementation of an environmental management system conforming to the international standard, structuring our continuous improvement approach.

law.title: Law 09-08
law.desc: Full compliance with Moroccan law on environmental protection, with regular inspections and independent audits.

cta.title: Discover our CSR report
cta.subtitle: Contact us to receive our detailed CSR report and learn more about our environmental initiatives

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Find answers to your questions about our products, services and the company

g1.a: Dakhla Aménagement S.A. is a clinker grinding center founded in 2015 in Dakhla, Morocco. With a production capacity of over 100,000 tonnes per year, we produce high-quality CPJ 45 and CPJ 55 cement for the Southern Moroccan market and Mauritania. Our plant is located in Lassargua, Dakhla district.

g2.a: You can reach us by phone at +212 658-265685, by email at contact@ciment-dam.com, or via our online contact form. Our sales team is available to answer all your questions and support you in your projects.

g3.a: We accept bank transfer, cheque and cash. Our standard terms are: 50% on order and 50% on delivery. Specific terms can be negotiated for long-term contracts and large volumes.

p1.q: What is the difference between CPJ 45 and CPJ 55?
p1.a: CPJ 45 offers a compressive strength of 45 MPa at 28 days, ideal for reinforced concrete, foundations and floor slabs. CPJ 55 offers a superior strength of 55 MPa, recommended for major civil engineering works and large infrastructure projects requiring exceptional performance.

p2.a: Our current rates are: CPJ 45 at 1,500 DH per tonne and CPJ 55 at 1,600 DH per tonne. Volume discounts apply from 10 tonnes. Companies receive an additional 3% discount and resellers 5%. Contact us for a personalized quote.

p3.a: We offer three packaging options: Bulk (tanker truck delivery with direct silo pumping), 50 kg bags (palletized, 40 bags per pallet) and industrial Big Bags of 1.5 tonnes. Bulk is recommended for large construction sites, offering the best economy and product preservation.

p4.a: Yes, our volume discounts are: 2% from 10T, 5% from 25T, 8% from 50T, 12% from 100T, and 15% from 500T. Companies receive an additional 3% discount and approved resellers 5%.

d1.a: You can order via our online quote form, by phone at +212 658-265685, by WhatsApp, or by email at contact@ciment-dam.com. Our sales team will respond within 24 hours with a personalized quote adapted to your project.

d2.q: What delivery zones do you cover?
d2.a: We deliver in the Dakhla region and within a 200 km radius: Dakhla City (free), Dakhla Region (+50 DH/T), Southern Morocco (+150 DH/T) and Mauritania (+250 DH/T). Express delivery is available for urgent orders.

d3.a: The standard delivery timeframe is 24 to 48 hours for Dakhla and its region. For Southern Morocco, allow 3 to 5 days, and 5 to 7 days for Mauritania. Urgent delivery reduces these timeframes by 30 to 50%.

d4.a: The minimum order is 10 tonnes for bulk and 1 pallet (2 tonnes or 40 bags of 50 kg) for bags. For Big Bags, the minimum order is 1 Big Bag (1.5 tonnes). Special arrangements can be discussed for specific construction sites.

t1.q: What strength is recommended for foundations?
t1.a: For standard foundations, CPJ 45 (45 MPa) is generally sufficient. For deep foundations or difficult soils, CPJ 55 (55 MPa) is recommended. In coastal areas like Dakhla, CPJ 55 offers better durability thanks to its denser and less permeable matrix. Consult our technical team for a personalized recommendation.

t2.q: How to store cement on site?
t2.a: Cement must be protected from moisture, stored on pallets in a covered and ventilated area. The shelf life is approximately 3 months for bagged cement in good conditions. For large quantities, silo storage is recommended. We offer bulk delivery with direct silo filling for optimal quality.

t3.a: Yes, CPJ 45 is perfectly suited for plastering and standard masonry. Its grinding fineness (≥ 350 m²/kg) ensures good workability and a smooth finish. For exterior plastering in coastal areas, we recommend CPJ 55 for its better resistance to sea spray and increased durability.

c1.a: Our cements are certified compliant with the Moroccan standard NM 10.1.004 and the European standard EN 197-1. ISO 9001 certification for quality management is in progress. Each batch is tested and accompanied by a certificate of conformity before shipment.

c2.q: Are you compliant with Moroccan standards?
c2.a: Yes, all our cements are produced in compliance with the NM 10.1.004 standard which defines cement specifications in Morocco, as well as the European standard EN 197-1. Each batch undergoes exhaustive laboratory testing: compressive strength, grinding fineness, setting time and stability.

c3.q: How is the quality of your products controlled?
c3.a: Each production batch undergoes rigorous testing in our laboratory: compressive strength at 2, 7 and 28 days, grinding fineness (Blaine surface), setting time, stability and heat of hydration. No product leaves the factory without a certificate of conformity. Our quality approach aims for ISO 9001 certification.

testimonials.title: Client Testimonials
testimonials.subtitle: Discover what our clients and partners say about Dakhla Aménagement

t1.name: Mohamed Ould Ahmed
t1.text: We have been working with Dakhla Aménagement since 2019 and the quality of CPJ 45 cement is consistent. Deliveries are on time and the sales team is very responsive. They are our reference supplier for all our residential projects in Dakhla.

t2.name: Fatima Zohra El Mansouri
t2.text: Dakhla Aménagement's CPJ 55 was our choice for the RN1 viaduct. The compressive strength is excellent and meets certifications. The technical team supported us throughout the project with professionalism.

t3.name: Karim Benali
t3.text: As a construction contractor, delivery reliability is crucial. DAM cement has never let us down. The 50 kg bag packaging is practical and quality is consistent with every order. A solid partner for Southern Morocco.

t4.name: Abderrahmane Dia
t4.text: As a reseller, the 5% discount and reliability of deliveries are a real plus. DAM cement is the most requested by our customers. The 50 kg bag packaging is practical and quality is consistently met with every order.

t5.name: Sophie Laurent
t5.text: I recommend CPJ 45 for plastering and finishing. The grinding fineness allows for smooth and durable surfaces. For seaside projects, CPJ 55 perfectly resists sea spray. A reliable partner for our projects in Dakhla.

t6.name: Hassan Mokhtar
t6.text: We deliver over 20 construction sites per year with DAM cement. The consistency of quality is remarkable — each batch meets specifications. The emergency delivery service has saved us several times. A trusted partner.

t7.name: Amina Taleb
t7.text: Dakhla Aménagement's grinding center is strategic for supplying the South. We used CPJ 55 for the foundations of our real estate program. Bulk delivery is efficient and the quality-price ratio is unbeatable.

t8.name: Youssef Khatri
t8.text: The partnership with Dakhla Aménagement is simple and efficient. Reseller rates are competitive, logistics are reliable and our sales contact is always available. It's a supplier that understands the needs of local distributors.

t9.name: Omar Ba
t9.text: For the construction of the new fishing port, DAM's CPJ 55 was decisive. The superior strength and bulk delivery allowed us to meet deadlines on such a demanding project. An exemplary collaboration from start to finish.

t10.name: Nadia Benchekroun
t10.text: For building my villa in Dakhla, I used CPJ 45 for all the masonry. The online quote is convenient and prices are transparent. Delivery was made the day after my order. I highly recommend!

durable.title: Sustainable Employment
durable.desc: Dakhla Aménagement S.A., founded in 2015, offers remarkable professional stability. Our continuous growth guarantees long-term career development prospects in the Dakhla region.

training.title: Continuous Training
training.desc: We invest in the development of our employees with technical, safety and management training programs. Each employee benefits from a personalized annual training plan.

social.title: Social Benefits
social.desc: Medical coverage, transportation, meals, productivity bonuses and regional benefits. We ensure the well-being of every team member.

impact.title: Local Impact
impact.desc: With 350 indirect jobs generated and a strong commitment to community development, every employee contributes directly to the growth of Southern Morocco.

dakhla.title: Living in Dakhla
dakhla.subtitle: An exceptional quality of life in the heart of Southern Morocco
dakhla.desc: Dakhla offers a unique living environment between the Atlantic Ocean and the Sahara Desert. With over 300 days of sunshine per year, a preserved coastline and a growing economy, the city attracts more and more talent. The community is warm, the cost of living is affordable and water sports — surfing, kitesurfing, fishing — are world-renowned.

press.title: Press Room
press.subtitle: Press releases, resources and media contacts — Dakhla Aménagement S.A.

r1.title: Production launch — DAM enters a new industrial era
r1.excerpt: Dakhla Aménagement announces the official launch of its CPJ 45 and CPJ 55 cement production at the Lassargua grinding center, marking a major milestone for the cement industry in Southern Morocco.

r2.title: DAM surpasses 100,000 tonnes of annual capacity
r2.excerpt: The Lassargua grinding center reaches a strategic milestone with increased production capacity to meet the growing demand of the Southern Moroccan and Mauritanian markets.

r3.title: ISO 9001 Certification — Towards quality excellence
r3.excerpt: Dakhla Aménagement initiates the ISO 9001 certification process for its quality management system, confirming its ambition to meet the most demanding international standards.

r4.title: New packaging line — Strategic investment
r4.excerpt: DAM inaugurates a new automated packaging line, strengthening its bagging and big bag capacity to meet the diverse needs of its 120 clients.

r5.title: Partnership with the Port of Dakhla — Optimized logistics
r5.excerpt: Dakhla Aménagement signs a strategic agreement with the Port Authority for the optimization of clinker reception and cement shipment, guaranteeing shortened delivery times.

investors.title: Investor Relations
investors.subtitle: Financial information, governance and growth prospects of Dakhla Aménagement S.A.

overview.title: DAM's Position
overview.text: Dakhla Aménagement S.A. holds a unique strategic position in the Moroccan cement market. As the only clinker grinding center in the Southern Provinces, the company meets a growing regional demand, driven by major infrastructure projects and the accelerated development of the Dakhla region.

highlights.title: Competitive Advantages
highlights.h1: Regional monopoly in cement production in the Southern Provinces
highlights.h2: Proximity to major construction sites: reduced lead times and logistics costs
highlights.h3: Market growth driven by the Southern Provinces Development Program
highlights.h4: NM 10.1.004 and EN 197-1 certifications ensuring standards compliance

pdg.desc: Drives the strategic vision and long-term development of Dakhla Aménagement S.A., ensuring value creation for all stakeholders.

dg.title: General Manager
